objectives: Conjugate regular and common irregular verbs in the imperfect
Use imperfect for habitual past actions and descriptions
Describe what things used to be like

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Abuela
dialogue_blocks.text: Cuando yo era joven, vivíamos en un pueblo pequeño.
dialogue_blocks.translation: When I was young, we lived in a small town.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈkwando jo ˈeɾa ˈxoβen ‖ biˈβiamos en um ˈpweβlo peˈkeɲo]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Abuela
dialogue_blocks.text: Era tranquila. Todos nos conocíamos y siempre nos ayudábamos.
dialogue_blocks.translation: It was peaceful. Everyone knew each other and we always helped each other.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈeɾa tɾaŋˈkila ‖ ˈtoðos nos konoˈθiamos i ˈsjempɾe nos aʝuˈðaβamos]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Nieta
dialogue_blocks.translation: What did you used to do as a child?

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Abuela
dialogue_blocks.text: Jugaba con mis primos, corría por los campos y leía muchos cuentos.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I used to play with my cousins, run through the fields and read many stories.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [xuˈɣaβa kon mis ˈpɾimos ‖ koˈria poɾ los ˈkampos i leˈia ˈmuʧos ˈkwentos]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Nieta
dialogue_blocks.translation: And what was your school like?

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Abuela
dialogue_blocks.text: La escuela era pequeña — solo había veinte estudiantes en mi clase.
dialogue_blocks.translation: The school was small — there were only twenty students in my class.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [la esˈkwela ˈeɾa peˈkeɲa ‖ ˈsolo aˈβia ˈβejnte estuˈðjantes en mi ˈklase]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Nieta
dialogue_blocks.text: ¡Me gustaría haber vivido en esa época!
dialogue_blocks.translation: I would have liked to have lived in that era!
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [me ɣustaˈɾia aˈβeɾ biˈβiðo en ˈesa ˈepoka]

exercises.instruction: Escoge la oración correcta (Choose the correct sentence)
exercises.question: Which sentence correctly uses the imperfect to describe a habitual past action?

options.text: Cuando era niño, comía tacos todos los domingos.
Cuando era niño, comí tacos todos los domingos.
Cuando era niño, como tacos todos los domingos.
Cuando era niño, comeré tacos todos los domingos.

feedback.correct: ¡Perfecto! The imperfect 'comía' expresses a repeated habitual action in the past.
feedback.incorrect: 'Comía' (imperfect) is correct for habitual past. 'Comí' (preterite) would mean one specific time. 'Como' is present, 'comeré' is future.

exercises.instruction: Une el uso del imperfecto con su ejemplo (Match the imperfect use to its example)

pairs.english: La casa era grande y bonita.

exercises.instruction: Completa con la forma correcta del imperfecto (Complete with the imperfect)

dialogue.speaker: Marcos
dialogue.text: De niño, mi familia y yo _____ al parque todos los domingos. (ir)

dialogue.speaker: Lucia
dialogue.text: ¡Qué bonito! Yo _____ con mis abuelos los fines de semana. (estar)

dialogue.speaker: Lucia
dialogue.text: Sí, siempre nos _____ historias antes de dormir. (contar)

speaking_rubric.title: Mi infancia (My Childhood)
speaking_rubric.scenario: Tell a friend about what your life was like when you were a child — where you lived, what you used to do, what school was like.
speaking_rubric.prompt: Describe cómo era tu vida de niño/a. Usa el imperfecto.

sample_response.text: Cuando era niña, vivía en un apartamento pequeño con mis padres. Iba a la escuela a pie todos los días. Los fines de semana jugaba con mis vecinos y veíamos películas juntos.
sample_response.ipa: [ˈkwando ˈeɾa ˈniɲa ‖ biˈβia en un apaɾtaˈmento peˈkeɲo ‖ ˈiβa a la esˈkwela a ˈpje]

cultural_notes.formality: Nostalgia is a common conversational topic in Latin America. Sharing childhood memories with '¿Te acuerdas cuando...?' (Do you remember when...?) is a warm social opener.
cultural_notes.gestures: Placing a hand over the heart while saying 'cuando era niño/a' signals emotional warmth and nostalgia.
cultural_notes.regional_variations: In Mexico, 'antes' (before/used to) is commonly added to emphasize the imperfect: 'Antes vivía en Guadalajara.' The verb 'soler' + infinitive can substitute for imperfect habit: 'Solía correr por las mañanas.'
